paragraphs.body: Traveling is a hobby that heals people's souls and fulfills their lives with amazing experiences. Sometimes people like to live through their loved ones or friends' travel experiences. You may wonder why people choose to ride on their loved ones' coattails when it comes to traveling. I don’t understand that myself. That is exactly what my mom does to me.

Recently I have read an article by Jonathan Look Jr. titled “Why Travel Is Important Today More Than Ever” in this article he insists that traveling impacts everyone’s life in a positive and meaningful way. However, the most important piece that I took from the article is the idea that travel is the antidote to our fears. For most people, they won’t agree. Many people will argue this idea because everyone has different fears and different emotions. He claims that when he hears people discussing their fear of traveling it is usually due to the experiences and stories told by others. After reading that I could only think of one person.

My mother, Rebecca, means a whole lot to me. Me and her did everything together until I left for college. We always went grocery shopping, cleaned, watched movies, cooked, baked, and everything in between. The only thing that we wouldn't do together would be fly on a plane with each other. We would go grocery shopping every Monday for about 3 hours going to many different stores. I remember her always asking me to get lunch with her at this one store, and we would joke about how expensive it is when it's cheap. My mom will always get a hot dog, and I would get a pretzel, cookie, and drink. All that and it would only cost us about 5 dollars for it all.
We would spend countless hours together no matter how tired we were or how busy.

As I mentioned, we never flew together when I would go away on trips. The last few years I would be on a plane every weekend for softball during the summer months. But it would either just be my dad and I or just me, all by myself...traveling the United States. When I would travel all by myself my mom would constantly text me to ask for pictures, videos, just everything I could see. The main thing she would ask for was pictures of the area I was in or videos of the plane landing or even videos of the beach, and of course I would respond back with, “Yes, of course mom. It's beautiful here.” Then I would send her the picture or video. When she texted me, I couldn’t help but wonder why she just didn’t come if she wanted to see all this stuff. It never made sense to me why she can spend all this time with me at home, but when a plane gets involved, she can’t. I would ask her why she didn’t want to, she always responded back with, “Honey, you don’t need me. You’re old and grown enough to do this all by yourself.” Yes, I knew I could do it, but I wanted her to experience these things with me. Mainly because I knew she never did when she was younger.

In the summer of 2022, I had to go to California for the very first time and I was so nervous. I was hoping my mom would go with me to help me prepare for softball and cheer me on. When I found out that she wasn’t going to go, I was devastated because I wanted her to experience this firsthand. Especially, when I knew it would be her very first time as well as mine, but sadly not. I wanted her to have this experience because I didn’t want her to live it through me anymore. I wanted her to feel happy, excited, and nervous just like I do when I get on the airplane and when we land. I swear if she just went on one trip with me it would heal her fear of traveling on an airplane. It would heal everything for her.
After reading Jonathan’s article about why traveling is so important and how afraid someone can be to experience it for themselves, I could not help but think of my momma.

According to Jonathan, “Travel is the antidote to our fears. It’s rare to meet a fearful traveler and it’s even more rare to meet one that grows as they gain more experience.” Basically, Jonathan is saying that traveling heals the mind, allowing development within yourself and a world of understanding. This also allows us to develop the ability to make a stronger and meaningful connection with others in the world. This information just makes me think about why my mom doesn’t want to travel. She has such a strong connection with me so why doesn't she want to have one with me when we travel as well. How can a person want to see something in the world, but not want to do it themselves? Jonathan states, “Traveling does not make you a better person, but neither does fear. Learning about this world gives us freedom over those who would use fears to cynically and selfishly influence and control us.” Jonathan’s point is that people shouldn’t let their fear control what they do. They should do what they want to do without fear. In these quotes I felt as if I was talking to my mom and convincing her to push her fear to the side and travel with me this one time.

To make sure that my point gets across to my mother and others. You shouldn’t let your fears, anxiety, or stress affect you from doing what you want to do when it comes to traveling. If you want to travel, then go travel and I promise you won’t regret it. The world is a beautiful, amazing, claimable place. You won’t forget the first time you stepped foot on the airplane and your first step off the plane. You will feel like a whole different person. Being able to travel makes you feel special. Traveling also helps you clear your mind. The main idea you should get from Jonathan’s article is that fear doesn’t have power over you. You have the power and control over yourself.

annotations.comment: The photos are great! They create an excellent tone and help clarify your essay's ideas :)
